summary: Mandibular teeth experience higher pressure and wear due to their role in bearing most masticatory force; this makes them particularly prone to toothbrush-induced abrasions when combined with typical brushing motions involving side-to-side movement. \boxed{} The summary captures the essential point about why mandibular teeth suffer more frequent abrasions related to brushing habits without extraneous detail beyond what was clinically significant for addressing the core query posed by the original statement/question.
